top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The evening news is dominated by escalating military friction: the US has launched fresh 'self-defence' strikes on missile launchers near Bandar Abbas after Iran shot down an MQ-9 drone, and Russia is openly warning of 'systematic strikes' on Kyiv that could target diplomatic personnel. On the economic side, Singapore's Q1 GDP beat hard at +6.0%, while SpaceX has formally set a US$1.75 trillion IPO for June 12. Closer to home, 50,000 Californians remain under evacuation orders as a chemical tank in Garden Grove teeters on the edge of explosion.

stories.headline: US strikes Iranian missile launchers near Bandar Abbas after MQ-9 drone shot down
stories.summary: CENTCOM has launched fresh 'self-defence' strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and mine-laying boats around the Strait of Hormuz, hours after Iran's Revolutionary Guard shot down a US MQ-9 Reaper drone and fired on an F-35 it forced to turn back. Tehran calls it a 'grave' ceasefire violation; Washington insists the April 8 ceasefire still holds while it tries to lock in a longer deal in Doha.

stories.headline: Russia warns of 'systematic strikes' on Kyiv, urges foreigners to evacuate
stories.summary: Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov has told US Secretary of State Marco Rubio that Russia will begin hitting 'decision-making centres' in Kyiv and urged Washington to evacuate its diplomats. The warning follows weekend strikes that used 90 missiles and 600+ drones — including a nuclear-capable Oreshnik hypersonic — and have prompted Ukraine's foreign minister to call on allies not to give in to 'Russian blackmail'.

stories.headline: Singapore Q1 GDP smashes estimates at +6.0% but trade ministry flags Middle East risks
stories.summary: Singapore's economy expanded 6.0% year-on-year in Q1 2026, more than double the early advance estimate of 4.6%, lifted by AI-driven electronics exports up 57.8%. The trade ministry kept its full-year forecast at 2–4% and warned that the US–Iran escalation around the Strait of Hormuz remains the single biggest downside risk to the rest of the year.

stories.headline: SpaceX files S-1 for US$1.75 trillion June 12 Nasdaq IPO under ticker SPCX
stories.summary: SpaceX has formally filed its S-1 with the SEC, targeting a Nasdaq debut on June 12 under ticker SPCX. Bankers led by Goldman Sachs are aiming for a US$1.75 trillion valuation and up to US$75 billion raised — which would dwarf Saudi Aramco's 2019 record. The company posted US$18.7 billion in revenue last year, up 33% on 2024.

stories.headline: Israeli airstrike kills 45 at Rafah displacement camp, sparks Egyptian border clash
stories.summary: An Israeli strike on the Tel al-Sultan displacement camp in Rafah has killed at least 45 people — mostly women and children — and triggered a rare cross-border skirmish in which one Egyptian soldier was killed. A second strike less than 48 hours later on the Al-Mawasi camp killed another 21. Israel says it was targeting two senior Hamas officials; the area was not under evacuation orders.

stories.headline: 50,000 still under evacuation orders as California chemical tank teeters on the edge
stories.summary: A storage tank holding up to 7,000 gallons of highly flammable methyl methacrylate at the GKN Aerospace plant in Garden Grove, California, remains in 'crisis' and at risk of spilling or exploding. Six Orange County cities — Garden Grove, Cypress, Stanton, Anaheim, Buena Park and Westminster — are affected by the evacuation orders, though authorities say worst-case explosion risk has now been reduced.

stories.headline: Bangladesh measles deaths climb past 528 children as outbreak spreads to 58 districts
stories.summary: More than 528 children have now died in Bangladesh's measles outbreak — 13 in the past 24 hours alone — with 60,000+ suspected cases across 58 of the country's 64 districts. The deadliest surge in decades is being blamed on a vaccine procurement collapse in 2024–25 under the interim government; an emergency mass-vaccination drive has so far reached 18 million children.

stories.headline: Iran restores full internet access after 87-day shutdown as Hajj draws 1.5M+ to Mecca
stories.summary: President Masoud Pezeshkian has ordered the rollback of nearly three months of internet restrictions, returning most Iranians to the open web for the first time in 87 days. Separately, over 1.5 million pilgrims have arrived in Mecca for the annual Hajj, gathering at Mount Arafat amid 40°C heat and a deeply fragile regional ceasefire.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: It's a strong evening for the economy and a tense one for the wallet: Q1 GDP came in well above forecast at +6.0% year-on-year, even as the trade ministry warned the Hormuz crisis could still bite later in 2026. COE premiums hit fresh highs in this month's second bidding, with Cat B nearing S$130k and commercial Cat C at an all-time peak. On the security beat, twelve people aged 17–28 will be charged for selling fraudulently registered SIM cards to scam syndicates, and an alleged insider-trading ring leader has been denied bail. Weather watch: short thundery showers expected on most afternoons through end-May.

stories.headline: Singapore Q1 GDP shocks at +6.0% as AI-fuelled electronics exports surge 57.8%
stories.summary: First-quarter GDP came in at +6.0% year-on-year — well above the +4.6% advance estimate and the strongest print in two years. Wholesale trade, manufacturing and finance led the charge, with non-oil domestic exports up 9.6% on AI-related electronics demand. The trade ministry kept the full-year forecast at 2–4% and flagged the Middle East as the biggest external risk.

stories.headline: COE Cat B nears S$130k, commercial Cat C hits all-time high of S$92,223
stories.summary: The second COE bidding exercise of May 2026 (closed 20 May) saw Category B (larger cars >1,600cc) close at S$129,501 — up S$3,265 — while commercial vehicle Cat C hit an all-time high of S$92,223 and the Open Cat E topped out at S$130,000. Cat A dipped marginally to S$124,229; Cat D (motorbikes) closed at S$9,689. Buyers are rushing to lock in supply before further squeezes.

stories.headline: 12 people aged 17–28 to be charged for selling fraudulently registered SIM cards
stories.summary: The Singapore Police Force says eight men and four women — aged between 17 and 28 — will be charged in court between 25–29 May over their suspected role in providing fraudulently registered postpaid SIM cards to a criminal syndicate. Members of the group were allegedly paid just S$10–20 per card. The cards were used to power scam operations targeting victims in Singapore and abroad.

stories.headline: Alleged leader of international insider-trading ring denied bail in Singapore High Court
stories.summary: A Singaporean accused by the US of running an international insider-trading ring has been denied bail by the High Court while fighting extradition. Justice Tay Yong Kwang ruled him a flight risk. US prosecutors allege the ring profited from material non-public information across multiple jurisdictions.

stories.headline: Singapore and China jointly urge Middle East ceasefire and safe maritime flows
stories.summary: Singapore and China have issued a joint call for an immediate Middle East ceasefire and emphasised the importance of safeguarding maritime trade routes, with the Strait of Hormuz now central to both economies' supply chains. The statement comes as US-Iran tensions flare and Singapore's trade ministry openly flags the Hormuz risk to its 2026 outlook.

stories.headline: NEA: short thundery showers expected on most afternoons through end-May
stories.summary: NEA's fortnightly outlook says short-duration thundery showers will hit parts of Singapore on most afternoons for the rest of May, occasionally extending into the evening. Sumatra squalls may bring widespread thundery showers and gusty winds in the pre-dawn hours on a few days. Daytime highs of 33–35°C are expected. Carry an umbrella — and watch for flash floods in low-lying areas.

stories.headline: NDP 2026 to be held at National Stadium — no Red Lions or mobile column this year
stories.summary: Mothership confirms this year's National Day Parade will take place at the National Stadium with a 42,000-seat civilian capacity — the largest in over a decade. Notable absences: the traditional Red Lions parachute jump and the mobile column. Heartland celebrations have also been scaled back to focus on a unified stadium-based experience.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: Tonight's picture: every active theatre is in escalation mode. The US and Iran are trading fire around the Strait of Hormuz despite an April 8 ceasefire still nominally in effect; Russia is openly threatening to hit Kyiv government buildings after its biggest combined missile-and-drone barrage of the war; and Israel has resumed heavy Rafah strikes, with civilian death tolls climbing and one Egyptian soldier killed in a border clash. Diplomats are talking in Doha, but the language out of Moscow and Tehran tonight is the language of retaliation, not de-escalation.

theatres.name: Russia–Ukraine War
theatres.status: Active — Russia threatens new 'systematic strikes' on Kyiv

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine, advancing on Kyiv, Kharkiv and the south.
Ukrainian counter-offensive stalls; war settles into a grinding, attritional front.
F-16s arrive in Ukrainian service; Russian glide-bomb and Shahed drone campaigns escalate.
Multiple rounds of peace talks open and collapse; Russia hits energy grid each winter.
Largest sustained drone barrage of the war hits Kyiv apartments, killing civilians.
Ukrainian drone strike on a college dorm in Russian-held Starobilsk kills at least 18 students.
Russia retaliates with 90 missiles and 600+ drones, including a nuclear-capable Oreshnik hypersonic; Kyiv's Chernobyl Museum and a historic market are destroyed; 4 dead, 87+ injured.
Belarusian opposition leader Tsikhanouskaya makes first official visit to Kyiv; 70+ foreign diplomats pay respects at strike sites.

Lavrov tells US Secretary of State Rubio that Moscow will begin striking 'decision-making centres' in Kyiv and asks the US to evacuate its diplomats; Ukraine's foreign minister urges allies not to bow to 'Russian blackmail'.

theatres.name: US/Israel–Iran Conflict (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Ceasefire under heavy strain — fresh US strikes, MQ-9 drone shot down

timeline.event: US and Israel launch coordinated strikes on Iranian nuclear and IRGC sites; Iran mines the Strait of Hormuz, threatening 20% of global oil.
Pakistan-brokered two-week US-Israel-Iran ceasefire takes effect.
Companion Lebanon ceasefire begins.
Iran's foreign ministry accuses US of repeated naval harassment of Iranian commercial vessels in Hormozgan.
Negotiations resume in Doha; Iran charges tolls of up to US$2M per ship for Hormuz transit.

CENTCOM strikes missile launchers and mine-laying boats near Bandar Abbas; IRGC says it shot down a US MQ-9 Reaper drone and forced an F-35 to turn back; Iran calls it a 'grave violation' of the ceasefire.

Iranian leadership warns Gulf monarchies will 'no longer shield' US bases; framework deal to extend ceasefire 60 days and reopen Hormuz reportedly close, though Rubio cautions any nuclear settlement 'can't be done in 72 hours'. President Pezeshkian also restores full internet access in Iran after 87 days.

theatres.name: Israel–Gaza / Lebanon
theatres.status: Escalation — Rafah strikes resume, Egyptian soldier killed

timeline.event: Hamas attack on Israel kills ~1,200 and takes 250+ hostages; Israel begins major military campaign in Gaza.
After a year-plus of war and ~70,000+ Palestinian deaths, a partial ceasefire is brokered to halt active hostilities.
Companion Lebanon ceasefire takes effect; Hezbollah and IDF agree to staged pullback.
Israeli strikes inside Gaza climb 35% month-on-month; Gaza health ministry reports 880 Palestinian deaths since the 'ceasefire' began, bringing total war casualties to 72,797.
Israeli airstrike on Tel al-Sultan displacement camp in Rafah kills at least 45 — mostly women and children — and an Egyptian soldier is killed in a rare cross-border clash.

Second strike on Al-Mawasi displacement zone kills 21; IDF resumes airstrikes on Mashghara and Sohmor in southern Lebanon, citing Hezbollah ceasefire violations, and orders residents north to the Beqaa Valley.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius Andes-virus cluster is creeping rather than exploding — 12 total cases (10 confirmed, 2 probable), 3 deaths, and just one new case since the previous ECDC update on 24 May. The ship is still docked in Rotterdam for sanitation, and the CDC is keeping 18 repatriated US passengers at the Nebraska Quarantine Facility through 31 May. ECDC continues to rate the risk to the general EU/EEA population as 'very low'. Bigger health concerns this cycle: DR Congo's Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak (now a WHO PHEIC at 1,010 cases) and Bangladesh's measles surge (528+ child deaths) — both unfolding in the background.

stories.headline: ECDC: MV Hondius hantavirus cluster steady at 12 cases (10 confirmed, 2 probable), 3 deaths
stories.summary: ECDC's 24 May update lists 12 total cases — 10 confirmed, 2 probable — and 3 deaths (2 of which have been laboratory-confirmed as Andes virus). Only one new case has been reported since the prior update, with no new fatalities. Cases now span 12 countries including Singapore, where one former passenger remains hospitalised. The EU/EEA population risk remains 'very low'.

stories.headline: CDC keeps 18 repatriated US passengers at Nebraska Quarantine Facility through 31 May
stories.summary: The CDC confirms that 18 US passengers repatriated from the MV Hondius will remain at the Nebraska National Quarantine Facility through 31 May 2026. Andes virus has an incubation period that can stretch past three weeks, which is why the precautionary hold extends beyond the typical 14-day window.

stories.headline: MV Hondius still in Rotterdam undergoing deep sanitation; source likely a pre-boarding land exposure
stories.summary: The Dutch-flagged expedition cruise ship remains docked in Rotterdam for sanitation. The current working hypothesis is that Patient Zero acquired the infection on land — before boarding — in Argentina or Chile, then transmitted aboard during the Antarctic-region voyage. Investigators continue to work with South American authorities to pin down the original exposure site.

stories.headline: WHO declares Bundibugyo-strain Ebola in DR Congo and Uganda a PHEIC at 1,010 cases
stories.summary: The Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak — declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern on 17 May — now stands at 1,010 suspected and confirmed cases and at least 231 deaths across DR Congo and Uganda. Two laboratory-confirmed cases have appeared in Kampala. Unlike the more common Zaire strain, no licensed vaccine exists for Bundibugyo, and three healthcare facilities have been attacked by armed civilians this past week.

stories.headline: Bangladesh measles outbreak: 528+ children dead, 60,000+ suspected cases across 58 districts
stories.summary: More than 528 children have now died in Bangladesh's measles outbreak — 13 in the past 24 hours — with 60,000+ suspected cases spreading across 58 of the country's 64 districts. The trigger was a vaccine procurement collapse under the interim government in 2024–25. An emergency mass-vaccination drive has so far reached 18 million children.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Two stories dominate tonight: a 9-year-old Linux kernel root-escalation flaw (CVE-2026-46333) for which public exploits are already circulating, and a CVSS-10.0 Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN auth-bypass (CVE-2026-20182) that has been exploited as a zero-day by a 'highly sophisticated' actor. Add to that the active Ghost CMS SQL-injection campaign (CVE-2026-26980) that has hijacked 700+ sites including Harvard and DuckDuckGo to push ClickFix malware, plus an Apache HTTP/2 double-free (CVE-2026-23918) enabling DoS and possible RCE. Patch fast — three of these are pre-auth or local-root and at least one is being weaponised at scale.

stories.headline: 9-year-old Linux kernel flaw lets any local user root major distros via ptrace race
stories.summary: Qualys disclosed a logic flaw in the Linux kernel's __ptrace_may_access() function that has been present since v4.10-rc1 (November 2016). Any unprivileged local shell can pair it with pidfd_getfd() to read /etc/shadow, lift SSH host private keys, or run arbitrary commands as root via four set-UID binaries (chage, ssh-keysign, pkexec, accounts-daemon). Working exploits are circulating publicly. Patch your kernel; as a stop-gap, set kernel.yama.ptrace_scope=2.

stories.headline: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN auth-bypass (CVSS 10.0) actively exploited in the wild
stories.summary: Cisco has patched a maximum-severity authentication-bypass in the Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager already being abused by what Cisco calls a 'highly sophisticated' threat actor. A remote, unauthenticated attacker can become an authenticated peer of the appliance and inject a public key into the vmanage-admin SSH authorized_keys file — full takeover. CISA has added it to the KEV catalog with a federal patch deadline of 17 May 2026.

stories.headline: Ghost CMS SQL injection (CVE-2026-26980) hijacks 700+ sites including Harvard, DuckDuckGo
stories.summary: A large-scale campaign is abusing an unparameterised ORDER BY clause in Ghost's Content API (v3.24.0–6.19.0) to steal admin API keys, then inject malicious JS that serves fake Cloudflare verification pages to push ClickFix malware (DLL loaders, JS droppers, an Electron payload 'UtilifySetup.exe'). 700+ sites compromised — universities, fintech, AI/SaaS, security blogs. Upgrade to 6.19.1+ and rotate every admin key and session token.

stories.headline: Apache HTTP/2 double-free (CVE-2026-23918) enables DoS and potential RCE
stories.summary: A double-free in Apache 2.4.66's mod_http2 (h2_mplx.c stream cleanup) triggers when a client sends an HTTP/2 HEADERS frame immediately followed by RST_STREAM. Reliable DoS is straightforward; on default Debian-derived builds using the mmap allocator, RCE is achievable by mmap-reusing the freed virtual address with a fake h2_stream pointing its pool cleanup to system(). Fixed in 2.4.67 — patch or disable HTTP/2.

stories.headline: Microsoft Exchange on-prem CVE-2026-42897 added to CISA KEV — XSS via crafted email
stories.summary: An Outlook Web Access spoofing/XSS bug in on-prem Exchange Server is being weaponised by sending a crafted email which, once previewed in OWA under specific conditions, runs attacker-supplied JavaScript in the victim's browser session. CISA has now placed it on the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, ordering federal agencies to patch.

stories.headline: Instructure (Canvas) reaches ransom 'agreement' with ShinyHunters over 3.65TB leak
stories.summary: Instructure has confirmed it reached an 'agreement' with ShinyHunters after the group stole 3.65TB of Canvas LMS data — about 275M records covering students, teachers and staff across 9,000+ institutions. A second-wave defacement of ~330 institution login portals on 7 May forced the deal. The incident is one of the largest education-sector breaches on record and is forcing a global re-think of LMS supplier risk.
